articles.title: Messi subs in and scores again as defending World Cup champ Argentina beats Jordan to sweep group
articles.description:
“I am very happy for him, for the moment he is having."

The post Messi subs in and scores again as defending World Cup champ Argentina beats Jordan to sweep group appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Argentina defeats Jordan, securing a spot in the knockout stage of the World Cup.
Lionel Messi scored after subbing in, showcasing his continued impact on the team.
Boston fans celebrate Messi's performance, enhancing local interest in the World Cup.

articles.title: Austria, Algeria trade goals in stoppage time, both advance at World Cup with stunning 3-3 draw
articles.description:
“I've been a coach for about 40 years. I don't remember a game that had such a dramatic course, and such an unexpected trajectory,” Austria coach Ralf Rangnick said, shaking his head.

The post Austria, Algeria trade goals in stoppage time, both advance at World Cup with stunning 3-3 draw appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Austria and Algeria played to a dramatic 3-3 draw in World Cup match.
Both teams advanced to the knockout stage after the June 28 match.
Local fans can expect heightened excitement as both teams progress in the tournament.

articles.title: Iran narrowly knocked out of tumultuous World Cup on Austria’s last-second goal
articles.description:
“What these young Iranian national team players have done should be recorded in history,” Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei said. “Why? Because the host treated us in the worst possible way.”

The post Iran narrowly knocked out of tumultuous World Cup on Austria’s last-second goal appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Iran's national team eliminated from World Cup by Austria's last-second goal.
Coach Amir Ghalenoei criticized host treatment of players during the tournament.
Local Iranian community in Boston reflects on team's historic performance despite challenges.
